name: Chocolate Raspberry Mousse Cake
recipeInstructions:
This recipe makes double the amount of cake needed, so you'll be able to freeze the extra cake to have on hand when you want to make an encore of these fabulous flavors.
CHOCOLATE CAKE: In a large bowl, beat egg yolks with 1/4 cup sugar until very pale. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until soft peaks form; gradually beat in remaining sugar until stiff peaks form. Fold the egg white mixture into the egg yolk mixture. Sift flour and cocoa over the egg mixture; gently fold in. Pour into a buttered 8-inch springform pan; bake at 350°F for 30-35 minutes or until the top springs back when lightly touched. Run a knife around the edge of the cake to loosen. Let cool on a wire rack.
RASPBERRY SYRUP: In a small saucepan, combine sugar and water; cook over medium heat until the sugar has dissolved, about 1 minute. Stir in liqueur. Let cool.
RASPBERRY MOUSSE: In a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatin over water; let stand for 5 minutes to soften. Pass berries through a food mill to puree and remove seeds to make about 2 cups. Transfer the puree to a separate saucepan. Add sugar and lemon juice; cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved, about 5 minutes. Stir in liqueur; transfer to a large bowl. Over low heat, heat gelatin until dissolved; stir into the raspberry mixture. Chill, stirring occasionally, over a larger bowl of ice and water for about 20 minutes or until it reaches the consistency of raw egg whites. Whip cream; fold into the cooled raspberry mixture. Reserve half for garnish.
TO ASSEMBLE: Cut the cake into 3 or 4 thin layers; reserve 2 layers and freeze the remaining layers for another use. Sprinkle 1 cut side of each of the 2 layers with raspberry syrup. Place 1 layer, syrup side up, in a 9-inch springform pan; pour in half of the raspberry mousse. Top with the second cake layer; pour in enough of the remaining mousse to come almost to the top of the pan. Smooth the surface. Refrigerate until firm, 1-2 hours.
CHOCOLATE GLAZE: In the top of a double boiler over hot, not boiling, water, melt chocolate with cream, stirring until smooth. Let cool to room temperature yet still spreadable; pour over the mousse and spread evenly. Spoon the remaining mousse into a pastry bag fitted with a small plain tip; pipe three concentric circles over the top of the cake. Pull a toothpick through the circles to form a spiderweb design. Refrigerate until chilled.
